params.text:
Two kinds of risk factor

Why one person falls and another does not usually comes down to a combination of factors. It helps to split these into two groups. Intrinsic factors come from within the person themselves:

  • Medication: some medicines, and especially several taken together, can cause drowsiness, dizziness or low blood pressure.
  • Eyesight: poor or uncorrected vision makes hazards hard to spot.
  • Continence: needing the toilet urgently, or often at night, leads to rushing.
  • Health conditions: dizziness, arthritis, a previous stroke, foot problems or confusion all affect stability.

Muscle weakness and poor balance also belong in this group, and a person who has already fallen is at greater risk of falling again.

Extrinsic factors and joined-up assessment

Extrinsic factors come from the environment and the things around the person:

  • Lighting: dim or glaring light hides trip hazards, especially on stairs and at night.
  • Footwear: loose slippers, worn soles or walking in socks give little grip or support.
  • Environment: clutter, trailing cables, loose rugs, wet floors and missing handrails.
  • Walking aids: a frame or stick of the wrong height, or left out of reach.

Because falls usually have several causes at once, good practice — recommended by NICE — is a multifactorial assessment. This considers all of a person’s risk factors together, intrinsic and extrinsic, and then puts in place a set of actions tailored to that individual. Tackling just one cause on its own rarely works; you address the whole picture.

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap to complete the summary of falls risk factors.
params.textField: Risk factors that come from within the person, such as *medication* side effects or poor eyesight, are called *intrinsic* factors. Those that come from the surroundings, such as poor *lighting* or a loose rug, are called *extrinsic* factors. Because a fall usually has several causes at once, NICE recommends a *multifactorial* assessment that considers them all together.
